name: Embedding Model Selection and Optimization
description: Choosing and optimizing embedding models for semantic search and RAG systems: general-purpose models (OpenAI text-embedding-3, Cohere, voyage-ai) vs. domain-specific fine-tuned embeddings, quantization (scalar, binary) for storage and speed, matryoshka representation learning for variable-dimension embeddings, and late interaction models (ColBERT) for precision. Model choice determines retrieval ceiling.
contrarian_take: MTEB leaderboard rankings are the dominant selection heuristic but MTEB is biased toward English academic text — rankings shift significantly on domain-specific corpora (code, legal, medical, informal web text). Fine-tuning a smaller model on in-domain data consistently beats using a larger general model at a fraction of the inference cost, but most teams never benchmark their specific data. Picking the top MTEB model is a reasonable default that is systematically suboptimal.
example_signals: ColBERT v2: Effective and Efficient Retrieval via Lightweight Late Interaction
